steps.title: Identify the roots from factored form
steps.content:
The function is given as $f(x) = (x-14)(x+19)$. Setting each factor equal to zero:
$x - 14 = 0 \Rightarrow x = 14$
$x + 19 = 0 \Rightarrow x = -19$
These are the two roots of the parabola.

steps.title: Find the minimum using the axis of symmetry
steps.content:
When a quadratic is written in factored form with a positive leading coefficient, the parabola opens upward. The minimum occurs at the vertex, which lies on the axis of symmetry—exactly at the midpoint of the two roots:
$$x = \frac{14 + (-19)}{2} = \frac{-5}{2}$$
That matches choice D — D is correct.
